steps.text: Submit written justification requesting an exception from the external peer review policy for extramural research.
Review and grant approval for the exception request in consultation with the Office of Financial Resources (OFR).
Conduct first-level review of extramural research applications by a panel of experts to evaluate scientific and technical merit.
Conduct second-level review by a separate senior advisory panel to evaluate preliminary recommendations in the context of program relevance, policy, and fiscal capacity.
Conduct first-step review of contract proposals by a technical evaluation panel (TEP) of experts.
Conduct second-step review of contract proposals to determine the competitive range and negotiate best and final offers.
Award the contract in consultation with the CIO director or designee.
Review and select task orders using existing OFR guidance and procedures.
Report planned and completed peer review activities annually to the Office of Science (OS).
Ensure reviews are conducted by experts external to CDC, not affiliated with the program, and without conflict of interest.

preconditions.text: In justified emergency situations where extramural research cannot go through standard external peer review.

constraints.text: If reviewers are recruited on an ad hoc basis, outside of a FACA committee, they must provide individual and independent comments of findings, and consensus decisions must be avoided.
